# Verify Startup Metric Inflation

## Claim being tested

Public growth metrics accurately represent business traction.

## Alternative hypothesis

Metrics are inflated, ambiguously defined, cherry-picked, or mixed across incompatible categories.

## Data fields

- ARR definition
- MRR by cohort
- signed contracts
- recognized revenue
- churn
- expansion/contraction
- refunds/discounts
- payment processor records
- customer count
- logo permissions
- audit/accounting support
- public claims timeline

## Metrics

- ARR/MRR consistency
- cohort retention
- revenue recognition vs contracted value
- customer concentration
- discount-adjusted revenue
- claim revision timeline
- press repetition network

## Verification logic

Compare public claims to:

- accounting definition
- cash receipts
- contract enforceability
- churn/retention
- time window
- whether future optional revenue is counted as current revenue

## Safe conclusion

"The public traction claim is underdefined or inconsistent with revenue-quality evidence."
